# npix_ellip_ann_exact { #astroapers.npix_ellip_ann_exact }

```python
npix_ellip_ann_exact(
    x,
    y,
    a_in,
    b_in,
    a_out,
    b_out,
    theta_in=0.0,
    theta_out=None,
    *,
    shape,
    mask=None,
    validate=True,
)
```

Return exact elliptical-annulus effective in-frame pixel counts.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

<code>[**x, y**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [scalar or array_like]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Aperture center coordinates in pixel units. Shapes must match after ``numpy.atleast_1d``. The return shape matches that broadcast-free input shape, so scalar inputs return one-element arrays.

<code>[**a_in, b_in, a_out, b_out**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Inner and outer ellipse semiaxes in pixels. Inner axes must be no larger than the corresponding outer axes, and at least one outer axis must be larger.

<code>[**theta_in**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float, optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [0.0]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Inner ellipse rotation angle in radians.

<code>[**theta_out**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [float or None, optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Outer ellipse rotation angle in radians. If `None`, uses ``theta_in``.

<code>[**shape**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [tuple[int, int]]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Image shape as ``(ny, nx)`` used to clip the aperture footprint.

<code>[**mask**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [array_like of bool, optional]{.parameter-annotation} [ = ]{.parameter-default-sep} [None]{.parameter-default}</code>

:   Boolean image mask with shape ``shape``. ``True`` pixels are excluded from the returned effective pixel count.

## Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

<code>[**npix**]{.parameter-name} [:]{.parameter-annotation-sep} [ndarray]{.parameter-annotation}</code>

:   Effective pixel count inside the image frame.

## Notes {.doc-section .doc-section-notes}

For matched inner and outer angles, this uses outer-minus-inner direct aperture summation. For split-angle annuli, it uses Rust-generated bbox-tight annulus weights and the same ``BoundingBox.apsum`` reduction.
